text: There is a kind of sterile stillness here, framed by glass and quiet electric lights. The reflected interior and the sealed room behind the wall evoke a sense of separation—am I outside looking in, or am I also enclosed by invisible walls of habit or expectation? Moving into the second scene, words leap out: 'I sense confusion and a lack of clarity.' Yes, my perception is blurred, the world shifting between control and chaos. It leaves me reflective, alert to the subtle boundary between seeing and truly understanding. Here, I feel curious yet slightly disoriented, compelled by the invitation to look deeper beneath surfaces.
